title: “There's A UFO On The Seafloor!” -Treasure Hunter Dennis Asberg
summary: Swedish treasure hunter Dennis Asberg discusses the Baltic Sea Anomaly, a 60-meter diameter circular object he discovered on the seafloor in June 2011. The object exhibits numerous anomalies including electromagnetic interference with equipment, unusual temperature readings, right-angle corridors, and appears to be detached from the seabed rather than being a geological formation. NATO ships have been observed monitoring the site, and Swedish military officials have denied involvement despite being filmed at the location.

acronyms.definition: An unidentified object found underwater, similar to a UFO but in aquatic environments
An underwater robot used for exploration and investigation

events.description: Dennis Asberg and Peter Lindberg discovered the Baltic Sea Anomaly on June 19th while searching for a shipwreck with champagne
Stefan Hogborn dove on the Baltic Sea Anomaly and touched it, discovering it was dark black underneath the sediment
Ocean X team discovered the Russian Tsar's submarine SO, built in 1904
Finnish colleagues joined expedition to Baltic Sea Anomaly and confirmed it appeared to be man-made construction

key_claims.claim: The Baltic Sea Anomaly is 60 meters in diameter and circular in shape with 90-degree angle corridors
The object is separated from the seabed floor based on sub-bottom profiling data, meaning it is not a geological formation
Equipment including GPS, ROVs, cameras, and lights malfunction when close to the object
The water temperature near the object was almost zero degrees Celsius instead of the normal 4-5 degrees
A 2-meter diameter hole in the object appears to be 'breathing' with sediment moving in and out
Lightning has struck the water directly above the object
Six to seven NATO ships from France, Germany, America, and Britain were observed monitoring the Ocean X crew at the site
Swedish military officials denied having visited the site despite being filmed there by Dennis Asberg
Remote viewers including Joseph McMoneagle and Angela Ford have analyzed the Baltic Sea Anomaly and suggested it is constructed and possibly an energy source
The object is too hard to take samples from with normal diving equipment
